title: [InstCombine] Use `m_NotForbidPoison` when folding `(X u< Y) ? -1 : (~X + Y) --> uadd.sat(~X, Y)`

tests.source_program:
define i32 @uadd_sat_poison_from_div_operand(i32 %x, i32 %y, i32 %z) {
  %div = udiv i32 %z, %z
  %notx = xor i32 %x, %div
  %a = add i32 %notx, %y
  %c = icmp ult i32 %x, %y
  %r = select i1 %c, i32 -1, i32 %a
  ret i32 %r
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i32 @uadd_sat_poison_from_div_operand(i32 %x, i32 %y, i32 %z) {
  %div = udiv i32 %z, %z
  %notx = xor i32 %x, %div
  %a = add i32 %notx, %y
  %c = icmp ult i32 %x, %y
  %r = select i1 %c, i32 -1, i32 %a
  ret i32 %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary covers poison in constant masks (e.g., <i32 -1, i32 poison>) but not poison propagating from variable operands through the XOR/NOT operation. This tests poison originating from a division operation flowing into the not pattern.

alive2.src:
define i32 @uadd_sat_poison_from_div_operand(i32 %x, i32 %y, i32 %z) {
  %div = udiv i32 %z, %z
  %notx = xor i32 %x, %div
  %a = add i32 %notx, %y
  %c = icmp ult i32 %x, %y
  %r = select i1 %c, i32 -1, i32 %a
  ret i32 %r
}

tests.source_program:
define <2 x i32> @uadd_sat_poison_in_variable_xor(<2 x i32> %x, <2 x i32> %y) {
  %mask = xor <2 x i32> <i32 -1, i32 poison>, <i32 0, i32 0>
  %notx = xor <2 x i32> %x, %mask
  %a = add <2 x i32> %notx, %y
  %c = icmp ult <2 x i32> %x, %y
  %r = select <2 x i1> %c, <2 x i32> %a, <2 x i32> <i32 -1, i32 -1>
  ret <2 x i32> %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions poison in constant mask but this test has poison propagated through variable XOR operation, testing poison handling in computed not-operand rather than literal constant.

alive2.src:
define <2 x i32> @uadd_sat_poison_in_variable_xor(<2 x i32> %x, <2 x i32> %y) {
  %mask = xor <2 x i32> <i32 -1, i32 poison>, <i32 0, i32 0>
  %notx = xor <2 x i32> %x, %mask
  %a = add <2 x i32> %notx, %y
  %c = icmp ult <2 x i32> %x, %y
  %r = select <2 x i1> %c, <2 x i32> %a, <2 x i32> <i32 -1, i32 -1>
  ret <2 x i32> %r
}

tests.source_program:
define i32 @uadd_sat_poison_from_select_operand(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %cond) {
  %x_maybe = select i1 %cond, i32 %x, i32 poison
  %notx = xor i32 %x_maybe, -1
  %a = add i32 %notx, %y
  %c = icmp ult i32 %x_maybe, %y
  %r = select i1 %c, i32 -1, i32 %a
  ret i32 %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary discusses poison in constants (like <i32 -1, i32 poison>) but not poison propagating from select operations into the variable operand X. This tests poison originating from control flow rather than constant masks.

alive2.src:

  %a = add i32 %notx, %y
  %c = icmp ult i32 %x_maybe, %y
  %r = select i1 %c, i32 -1, i32 %a
  ret i32 %r
}
